steps.title: Backsolve by testing choice D
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice D (84): If $12x = 84$, then $x = 7$.

Substitute $x = 7$ into the original equation:

Left side: $4(2(7) + 11) - 2(2(7) - 5) = 4(25) - 2(9) = 100 - 18 = 82$

Right side: $-9 + 13(7) = -9 + 91 = 82$

Both sides equal 82, confirming the solution. That matches choice D — D is correct.
